llm_proof_rationale: Invoke `K::cmp_properties()` / `K::zero_properties()` to put the cmp/zero axioms in scope, then prove the implication by case-splitting on whether the two candidate keys k1=keys@[r1] and k2=keys@[r2] differ: when iter is end, instantiate the glb-spec's `self@.contains_key(k)` forall for both glb1/glb2 to derive k1<k2 and k2<k1 (asymmetry contradiction); when iter is non-end, pick the gap-witness `hi` via `choose` and show that the larger of k1,k2 sits in the open interval (smaller_glb, hi), which the gap forall rules out — yielding k1==k2. Sortedness of `self_.keys@` then collapses index equality.
